audit_status.source_base: Greek Colossians text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for thanksgiving, prayer, image of invisible Divine, firstborn, all things through/in/for Messiah, reconciliation, mystery, Messiah in you.

verses.restored: Paul, apostle of Messiah Jesus through the will of the Divine, and Timothy the sibling,
to the holy and trusting siblings in Messiah at Colossae: grace to you and peace from Divine our Father.
We give thanks to the Divine, Father of our Lord Jesus Messiah, always praying concerning you,
having heard of your trust in Messiah Jesus and the love that you have toward all the holy ones,
because of the hope laid away for you in the heavens, which you heard before in the word of truth, the good news,
which has come to you, just as also in all the world it is bearing fruit and growing, as also among you from the day you heard and truly knew the grace of the Divine.
Just as you learned from Epaphras, our beloved fellow servant, who is a trustworthy servant of Messiah on our behalf,
who also made clear to us your love in Spirit.
Because of this, from the day we heard, we also do not cease praying for you and asking that you may be filled with the knowledge of his will in all wisdom and spiritual understanding,
to walk worthily of the Lord into all pleasing, bearing fruit in every good work and growing in the knowledge of the Divine,
being empowered with all power according to the strength of his glory, into all endurance and patience with joy,
giving thanks to the Father, who made you sufficient for the share of the inheritance of the holy ones in light,
who rescued us from the authority of darkness and transferred us into the reign of the Son of his love,
in whom we have redemption/release, the forgiveness/release of sins/misalignments.
He is image of the invisible Divine, firstborn of all creation,

because in him all things were created, in the heavens and upon the earth, the visible and the invisible, whether thrones or lordships or rulers or authorities; all things have been created through him and into him.
And he is before all things, and in him all things hold together.
And he is head of the body, the assembly; he is beginning, firstborn from the dead, so that he himself might come to be first in all things,
because in him all the fullness was pleased to dwell,
and through him to reconcile all things into himself, making peace through the blood of his cross, through him, whether things upon the earth or things in the heavens.
And you, once being alienated and hostile in mind by harmful works,
yet now he reconciled in the body of his flesh through death, to present you holy and blameless and unaccused before him,
if indeed you remain in the trust, founded and firm, and not moved away from the hope of the good news that you heard, proclaimed in all creation under heaven, of which I, Paul, became servant.
Now I rejoice in sufferings on behalf of you, and I fill up what is lacking of the pressures of Messiah in my flesh, on behalf of his body, which is the assembly,
of which I became servant according to the stewardship/administration of the Divine given to me for you, to fulfill the word of the Divine,
the mystery hidden from the ages and from the generations, but now made manifest to his holy ones,
to whom the Divine willed to make known what is the riches of the glory of this mystery among the nations: Messiah in you, the hope of glory.
Him we proclaim, warning every human and teaching every human in all wisdom, so that we may present every human complete/mature in Messiah.
For this also I labor, struggling according to his working that works in me in power.

translation_notes: The Christ hymn is cosmic: Messiah is image, firstborn, creator-context, sustainer, head, fullness, and reconciler of all things.
Firstborn of all creation is rank/source/heir language within the hymn, not a reduction of Messiah to a created being.
Reconciliation is cosmic and embodied: peace through the cross, in the body of his flesh.
